# Home of the Rainbowfish — Maintenance Manual

How to update, rebuild, and publish the **Home of the Rainbowfish** website
(the `unmack-fish-page` job). Read this before editing anything.

---

## 1. The golden rule

**Everything on the site is generated by one script:
`generator/build.py`, which writes the whole site into `src/`.**

Do **not** hand-edit files in `src/` — they are overwritten on every build.
To change the site, edit `build.py` (or the helper scripts it calls) and
rebuild. Think of `src/` as disposable output, not source.

```
generator/build.py   ──run──►   src/   ──serve──►   website
   (source of truth)             (generated, git-ignored)
```

---

## 2. Prerequisites (one-time setup)

You need **Python 3** and three packages:

| Package    | Import name | Used for                          |
|------------|-------------|-----------------------------------|
| PyMuPDF    | `fitz`      | reading the source PDF + images   |
| Pillow     | `PIL`       | saving / converting figures       |
| openpyxl   | `openpyxl`  | reading `HotRF.headings.xlsx`     |

Install them:

```bash
pip install PyMuPDF Pillow openpyxl
```

You also need the **source book PDF** in place (it is *not* stored in git,
because of its size):

```
jobs/unmack-fish-page/assets/source/Rainbowfishes.2011.pdf
```

If it is missing, restore it there before building. The new-species
articles live in `New_species_accounts/<name>/` and *are* in git.

---

## 3. Build and preview (the everyday loop)

From the job's `generator/` folder:

```bash
cd jobs/unmack-fish-page/generator
PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python build.py
```

`build.py` **clears and rebuilds `src/` from scratch** every time. A full
build takes a minute or two and prints a summary (e.g. "Wrote 879 files").

Preview locally — from `src/`:

```bash
cd ../src
python -m http.server 8000
```

Then open <http://127.0.0.1:8000/>. Rebuild, then just refresh the browser
to see changes. `PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8` matters on Windows so non-ASCII
characters don't crash the build.

---

## 4. Where things are

```
jobs/unmack-fish-page/
├── generator/
│   ├── build.py            ← THE generator (site chrome, book accounts, sections, index)
│   ├── extract_figs.py     ← renders all NEW-species figures from the FOS PDFs
│   ├── make_*.py           ← one per new species/group (jakora, garylangei, kiunga, mangrove, malanda)
│   └── crossref_lookup.py  ← one-off: fills references_crossref.json (DOIs)
├── assets/
│   ├── source/             ← the book PDF (NOT in git)
│   ├── converted/          ← extracted page text + images (regenerable, NOT in git)
│   ├── reference/live_site/ ← preserved old-site HTML (used for species order/citations)
│   ├── site_assets/        ← banner, home-page images, etc.
│   ├── references_raw.json        ← 517 extracted references
│   └── references_crossref.json   ← DOI + title lookups (cached)
├── New_species_accounts/   ← source PDFs for species added since the 2011 book
├── HotRF.headings.xlsx     ← authoritative heading ORDER (two columns)
├── src/                    ← GENERATED SITE (NOT in git — rebuild it)
├── brief.md                ← the job brief / decisions
└── MANUAL.md               ← this file
```

---

## 5. How the site is assembled (mental model)

`build.py`'s `main()` runs these stages in order:

1. **Chrome** — `render_page()` wraps every page in the banner, the left
   menu (from the `SECTIONS` list), breadcrumb and footer. Change the menu
   or page shell here.
2. **Book species accounts** — `locate_accounts()` finds each species in
   the PDF by its title font size; `build_species_account()` renders it
   verbatim with all figures (via `extract_account()`). 97 species +
   *M. splendida* subspecies + the undetermined page.
3. **New-species accounts** — `extract_figs.py` renders the figures, then
   `make_garylangei/jakora/kiunga/mangrove/malanda.py` write the HTML.
   These are run automatically from `main()` via `runpy`.
4. **Species index pages** — `build_sections()` → `species_index_html()`
   builds the "Rainbowfish Species" and "Blue Eye Species" list pages,
   linking every account.
5. **Other sections** — `build_section_multipage()` renders the verbatim
   text sections (Introduction, Distribution & Habitat, … Sources) from the
   `SECTION_BUILD` list; `build_background()` renders the front matter.
6. **Sources DOIs** — reference paragraphs get a doi.org link appended
   (see §9).

Styling lives in the `CSS` string inside `build.py`.

---

## 6. Common edits

### Change the menu, colours, header or footer
Edit `render_page()` (chrome), the `SECTIONS` list (menu items + order),
or the `CSS` string in `build.py`. Rebuild.

### Fix wording/figures in a **book** species account
The book accounts are extracted verbatim from the PDF, so most fixes are to
the *extraction logic* in `extract_account()` / `build_species_account()`,
not to text. If a single account misbehaves, prefer a **scoped** fix keyed
to that species (see the `Pseudomugil signifer` and
`Cairnsichthys rhombosomoides` special-cases already in the code) rather
than changing global behaviour, which risks breaking the other ~100
accounts. Always rebuild and spot-check several unrelated accounts after
any change to the shared extraction path.

### Edit a **new-species** account's text
Edit its `make_<name>.py` script (plain original prose in Python strings),
then rebuild. Caption text is passed through `html.escape()`, so put plain
characters there — **not** HTML entities like `&mdash;` (they'd become
`&amp;mdash;`). Inside paragraph bodies you *may* use entities.

---

## 7. Adding a NEW species account (full recipe)

This is the most common substantial task. Follow it end-to-end.

**Rules of the road**
- New-species prose is **written in our own words** from the source
  articles — never copy their text verbatim (unlike the book, we don't hold
  those articles' rights).
- If the species is **undescribed**, present it as such: genus-only italics
  (`Melanotaenia sp. "Name"`), no author citation, and say plainly it is not
  yet formally described.
- Where sources conflict, **defer to the most recent / most authoritative
  publication**. If identity is in doubt, leave the information out.

**Steps**

1. **Drop the source PDF(s)** into `New_species_accounts/<name>/`.

2. **Read the content** to write the account:
   ```bash
   python -c "import fitz; d=fitz.open('PATH.pdf'); print(d[PAGE].get_text('text'))"
   ```

3. **Find the figures** you want (page + xref + size):
   ```bash
   python -c "import fitz; d=fitz.open('PATH.pdf'); p=d[PAGE-1]; \
   [print(im['xref'], [round(x) for x in im['bbox']]) for im in p.get_image_info(xrefs=True)]"
   ```

4. **Render the figures** — add `render(...)` lines to `extract_figs.py`.
   **Always render via pixmap page-clip** (this is what `render()` does) —
   *never* `extract_image()`, because some FOS PDFs store their images
   upside-down and rely on the page to flip them (that was the cause of the
   "figures upside down" bug). Send Melanotaenia images to `RF`
   (`rainbowfish_species/`) and Kiunga/Pseudomugil to `BE`
   (`blue_eye_species/`).

5. **Write the HTML** — create `make_<name>.py` (copy an existing one as a
   template). It uses `build.render_page(...)` so the chrome matches, builds
   a list of `fig()/h2()/p()` parts, and writes to the right section folder.
   Blue-eyes use `active_slug="blue_eye_species"`; rainbowfishes use
   `"rainbowfish_species"`.

6. **Run it automatically** — add `"make_<name>"` to the `runpy` loop in
   `build.py`'s `main()` (the list that already contains
   `extract_figs, make_garylangei, …`).

7. **Register it in the index** — add a row to the `NEW_SPECIES` list in
   `main()`:
   ```python
   ("Rainbowfish Species", "Melanotaenia", "epithet", "melanotaenia_epithet.html",
    "Author, Year"),
   ```
   Use section `"Blue Eye Species"` for blue-eyes. For an undescribed form,
   set the epithet to `sp. "Name"` and leave the citation `""`. This links
   the account and slots it alphabetically within its genus via
   `_insert_species()`.

8. **Rebuild, verify, preview.** Check the account renders, the images
   appear (no broken links), and it shows up linked in the correct index
   page and genus position.

9. **Commit** (see §10).

---

## 8. Adding / editing the verbatim text sections
Non-species sections are declared in the `SECTION_BUILD` list in `main()`
(title, slug, divider page, PDF page range). `build_section_multipage()`
turns the page range into a landing page + one sub-page per subsidiary
heading (subsidiary headings and their order come from
`HotRF.headings.xlsx`). Front matter is `build_background()`.

---

## 9. References and DOIs (Sources page)
- `crossref_lookup.py` is a **one-off** batch: it reads
  `references_raw.json`, queries CrossRef, and caches results (validated by
  year + author surname) in `references_crossref.json`. It is resumable —
  rerun it to fill in more matches; it skips ones already cached.
- During the build, `_enrich_reference()` appends a `doi.org` link to each
  Sources-page reference that has a confident match, keyed by a
  punctuation-free author+year+title signature.
- **Titles are deliberately left as the book printed them** (CrossRef's were
  often messier). Don't "improve" this without checking the trade-off.

---

## 10. Publishing (git)
The repo is **`biomatix/Claude_Sandpit_Web_Pages`**. Committed: the
generators, briefs, reference JSON, site assets, and the new-species source
PDFs. **Git-ignored** (rebuildable or too large): `src/`, `assets/converted/`,
and the book PDF in `assets/source/`.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "…describe the change…"
git push origin main
```

Because `src/` is not committed, a fresh checkout reproduces the whole site
by running `build.py` — provided the book PDF is restored to
`assets/source/`.

---

## 11. Deploying to the web server (Digital Pacific / cPanel)

The site is **100% static** — plain HTML, CSS and images, no database or
server-side code — so it is served by copying the **contents of `src/`** into
a folder under the hosting account's `public_html`. It uses **relative
links**, so it works unchanged at the domain root *or* in any subfolder.

### 11.1 The live deployment (as configured)

- **Host / provider:** Digital Pacific, cPanel account `angfa`, primary
  domain **angfa.org.au**.
- **FTPS:** host `vmcp62.digitalpacific.com.au`, port 21, **explicit TLS**.
  (The shared TLS cert doesn't match that hostname, so an FTPS client must be
  told *not* to verify the cert; the account's own site cert on
  `angfa.org.au` is valid.)
- **Account home:** `/home2/angfa`; web root `/home2/angfa/public_html`
  (this holds the **live ANGFA Joomla site — do not touch it**).
- **This site lives in a subfolder:** `public_html/horf_test/` →
  **https://angfa.org.au/horf_test/**.

> **Credentials are NOT stored in this repo.** The cPanel/FTP password is held
> separately; supply it to the scripts below via an environment variable
> (`FTPPASS`). Rotate it if it is ever exposed.

### 11.2 Find the account's absolute path (needed for password protection)

Basic-Auth needs the *filesystem* path of the auth file. Get it from the
cPanel API (returns e.g. `/home2/angfa/public_html/horf_test`):

```bash
curl -sS -k -u "angfa:$FTPPASS" \
  "https://vmcp62.digitalpacific.com.au:2083/execute/Fileman/list_files?dir=%2Fpublic_html&types=dir"
```

### 11.3 Upload the site (FTPS, resumable)

Rebuild first (`python build.py`), then run a recursive FTPS uploader. Python's
built-in `ftplib` (FTP over explicit TLS) is the most reliable tool here — no
extra software needed. The uploader below **mirrors `src/` into
`public_html/horf_test/`**, creates directories as needed, **skips files whose
size already matches** (so it is resumable and fast for re-deploys), and
retries with reconnect on transient TLS data-channel hiccups (a few are
normal). ~200 MB / ~880 files takes roughly 7 minutes.

```python
# upload_site.py  —  run as:  FTPPASS='…' python -u upload_site.py
import os, ssl, ftplib, time
from pathlib import Path
HOST='vmcp62.digitalpacific.com.au'; PWD=os.environ['FTPPASS']
SRC=Path(r"…\jobs\unmack-fish-page\src")     # local build output
BASE='public_html/horf_test'                 # remote target folder
def connect():
    ctx=ssl.create_default_context(); ctx.check_hostname=False; ctx.verify_mode=ssl.CERT_NONE
    f=ftplib.FTP_TLS(HOST, timeout=120, context=ctx); f.login('angfa',PWD); f.prot_p(); return f
f=connect()
files=[]; dirs=[]
for p in sorted(SRC.rglob('*')):
    rel=p.relative_to(SRC).as_posix()
    (dirs if p.is_dir() else files).append((rel if p.is_dir() else (str(p),rel,p.stat().st_size)))
for d in dirs:
    try: f.mkd(BASE+'/'+d)
    except Exception: pass
for lp, rel, size in files:
    remote=BASE+'/'+rel
    try:
        if f.size(remote)==size: continue          # already uploaded, skip
    except Exception: pass
    for attempt in range(4):
        try:
            with open(lp,'rb') as fh: f.storbinary('STOR '+remote, fh); break
        except Exception:
            try: f.quit()
            except Exception: pass
            time.sleep(3); f=connect()
f.quit()
```

Alternative for a one-off or non-technical upload: zip `src/`'s contents,
upload the zip via **cPanel File Manager**, and "Extract" it inside the target
folder.

### 11.4 Keep it private (password protection)

To restrict the folder to reviewers, put an `.htaccess` + `.htpasswd` **inside
the folder** (self-contained — do *not* modify the account-level
`.htpasswds`). Create the password hash with `openssl passwd -apr1 'THEPASS'`,
write it to `.htpasswd` as `user:HASH`, and upload both files. `.htaccess`:

```apache
DirectoryIndex index.html
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine Off          # ignore the parent Joomla rewrite rules
</IfModule>
ErrorDocument 401 default  # IMPORTANT: restore the real 401 login prompt …
ErrorDocument 403 default  # … otherwise the parent site's error handling
ErrorDocument 404 default  # … swallows the 401 into a Joomla 404 page
AuthType Basic
AuthName "HORF review - private"
AuthUserFile /home2/angfa/public_html/horf_test/.htpasswd
Require valid-user
```

Verify: no credentials → **401** (`WWW-Authenticate` header present); correct
credentials → **200**. `.htpasswd` itself must return **403** (Apache blocks
`.ht*`).

### 11.5 Make it public

To open the folder to everyone, **remove the auth** — upload an `.htaccess`
with the auth lines stripped (keep the rest), and delete `.htpasswd`:

```apache
DirectoryIndex index.html
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine Off
</IfModule>
ErrorDocument 401 default
ErrorDocument 403 default
ErrorDocument 404 default
```

Verify: no credentials → **200**. To let search engines index it, also change
`src/robots.txt` from `Disallow: /` to allow (it is currently set to *no
index*, so the site is public-by-link but not in Google). A tidier public URL
(rename the folder, or a `rainbowfish.angfa.org.au` subdomain) works with the
same files — the relative links don't care about the path.

### 11.6 Notes / gotchas for the server
- **Case-sensitivity:** Linux treats `Foo.JPG` ≠ `foo.jpg`. Our slugs are all
  lowercase and links match — don't rename files by hand.
- **Confine writes to the site's own folder.** The live ANGFA Joomla site
  shares `public_html`; only ever create/modify files inside `horf_test/`
  (or whatever folder this site occupies).
- **HTTPS** is automatic via cPanel AutoSSL on `angfa.org.au`.
- **Rights check before public launch.** Book text/figures are republished
  under ANGFA's confirmed rights; the new-species accounts use **figures from
  third-party *Fishes of Sahul* articles** (credited in captions). Confirm the
  authors/ANGFA are happy for those to appear publicly.

I can't upload for you (it needs the hosting credentials), but I can produce
a ready-to-upload **zip of `src/`** and a per-release checklist on request.

---

## 12. Troubleshooting & gotchas

| Symptom | Cause / fix |
|---|---|
| **Figures upside-down / flipped** | You used `extract_image()`. Render via **pixmap page-clip** instead (see `extract_figs.py`'s `render()`). |
| **`&amp;mdash;` etc. showing literally** | An HTML entity was put inside a string that gets `html.escape()`d (e.g. a figure caption). Use a plain character there, or move the entity into a non-escaped paragraph body. |
| **A species missing from the index** | It isn't in `NEW_SPECIES` (new) or the book TOC. Add it to `NEW_SPECIES` in `main()`. |
| **Whole binomial italic for an undescribed form** | Use epithet `sp. "Name"` — `species_index_html()` then italicises the genus only. |
| **One book account's headings/attribution garbled** | Font-size heading detection edge case. Fix it **scoped to that species** (see existing `signifer` / `Cairnsichthys` special-cases); don't change global logic. |
| **Build crashes on Windows with a UnicodeEncodeError** | Prefix the command with `PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8`. |
| **Edit tool keeps failing on `build.py`** | Historically fixed by applying changes with throwaway Python patch scripts; whitespace in the file can be fiddly. |
| **Changes not showing** | You edited `src/` by hand (gets overwritten) — edit `build.py`/`make_*.py` and rebuild. |

---

## 13. Quick reference

```bash
# Rebuild the whole site
cd jobs/unmack-fish-page/generator && PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python build.py

# Preview
cd ../src && python -m http.server 8000        # http://127.0.0.1:8000/

# Refresh CrossRef DOIs (optional, resumable)
cd ../generator && python crossref_lookup.py

# Publish
git add -A && git commit -m "…" && git push origin main
```

Key files to edit: **`build.py`** (chrome, menu, CSS, book accounts,
sections, index) · **`extract_figs.py`** + **`make_*.py`** (new species) ·
**`HotRF.headings.xlsx`** (section/subsidiary order).
